name: Fried Tomato Slices with Curry Mint Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup 1% low-fat milk, or more as needed
1 large firm tomato, red or green
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the ingredients for the sauce in a small bowl. Set aside.
2. In a separate bowl, mix the Bisquick, cornmeal, and spices. Add the egg substitute and milk. Whisk until combined and airy. Set aside.
3. Cut the tomato into 1/3-inch thick slices (about 6 slices).
4. Heat a flat griddle over medium-high heat; coat the surface with cooking spray.
5. Dip each slice of tomato into the batter, allowing excess to drain.
6. Place the battered tomato slices on the pan. Cook for about 6 minutes on one side and 4 minutes on the other, or until browned without burning. Reduce heat if necessary.
7. Garnish with basil. Serve with the sauce.
[Per Serving: 164 cals, 2g fat (13% of calories), 272mg sodium, 27g carbs]
[MENU: Baby Bok Choy Steamed with Orange Zest and Ginger Fried Tomato Slices with Curry Mint Sauce 1+1/2 cup Cooked basmati rice 4 tablespoons pistachio nuts, dry-roasted 1 whole orange, cut into wedges]
